heading: Limitations
body:
- Atlas doesn't support Encryption at Rest using CMK (customer-managed key) over
  private endpoints for multi-cloud deployments. If you enable encryption
  at rest using CMK (customer-managed key) over Azure Private Link in an existing project with
  multi-cloud clusters, Atlas disables the multi-cloud
  clusters in your project.

- Atlas doesn't support Encryption at Rest using CMK (customer-managed key) over
  private endpoints for projects in the `INACTIVE` state.

- Once you've [Enable and Set up Private Endpoint Connections for a Project](azure-kms-enable-pvt-endpoint) to your AKV (Azure Key Vault), you can't modify
  the **Key Vault Name**, **Subscription ID**, or **Resource Group Name** of your AKV (Azure Key Vault), but you can still [rotate the Microsoft Azure Key Identifier](rotate-azure-key-identifier).
  To modify these values, you must first [remove](azure-remove-reject-pvt-endpoint).
